application_summary.name: Streamlit Calculator App
application_summary.purpose: A single-page web application that lets users perform the four basic arithmetic operations (addition, subtraction, multiplication, division) through a clean browser-based UI built with Streamlit.
key_functionalities: Accept two floating-point numbers from the user
Support Add, Subtract, Multiply, and Divide operations via a dropdown selector
Compute and display the arithmetic result on form submission
Guard against division-by-zero with a user-facing error message
Expose a collapsible expander section with structured computation details
application_summary.architecture_pattern: Single-file script, reactive Streamlit model (re-runs on every user interaction)

files.summary: Main entry-point for the Streamlit calculator: renders the UI form, executes arithmetic operations, enforces business rules (division-by-zero guard), and displays the result.
files.description: app.py is the sole source file of the application and contains both the UI layout logic and the arithmetic business rules. It defines a two-column numeric input form, a dropdown for selecting the desired operation, and a submit button. On submission it evaluates the chosen operation against the two inputs, enforces the division-by-zero constraint, and renders the result together with a collapsible detail panel. The file follows Streamlit's reactive execution model where the entire script re-runs on every user interaction.
business_rules: Division by zero is forbidden: when the Divide operation is selected and the second number equals zero, an error is shown and execution is halted immediately via st.stop().
Exactly four arithmetic operations are supported: Add, Subtract, Multiply, Divide.
The default selected operation is Add (index=0 in the selectbox).
Both number inputs default to 0.0 and accept up to six decimal places (format='%.6f').
Computation results are only produced and displayed after the form is explicitly submitted via the 'Calculate' button.
The result is presented in a human-readable expression format: '<num1> <symbol> <num2> = <result>'.
Computation details (first_number, second_number, operation, result) are available in a collapsible expander labelled 'Computation details'.

method_breakdown.summary: Configures the Streamlit page title, icon, and layout before any UI is rendered.
method_breakdown.significance: Establishes the page identity (title 'Calculator', icon '🧮', centered layout) shown in the browser tab and controls the overall layout width.

method_breakdown.summary: Renders the application title and descriptive caption at the top of the page.
method_breakdown.significance: Provides the user with context about the application's purpose.

method_breakdown.summary: Defines the interactive input form: two number inputs placed in side-by-side columns, an operation selector, and a submit button.
parameters: num1 (float): First operand, defaults to 0.0, precision 6 decimal places
num2 (float): Second operand, defaults to 0.0, precision 6 decimal places
method_breakdown.returns: submitted (bool): True when the 'Calculate' button is clicked
method_breakdown.significance: Central UI component that captures all user inputs required to perform a calculation. Uses st.form to batch all widget state into a single submission event, preventing partial re-runs.

method_breakdown.summary: Evaluates the selected arithmetic operation on the two input numbers and enforces the division-by-zero business rule.
parameters: submitted (bool): Guard flag from form submission
method_breakdown.returns: result (float): Computed arithmetic result; execution is halted before this point if division by zero is detected
method_breakdown.significance: Implements all four arithmetic operations and enforces the critical division-by-zero constraint. Uses st.stop() to safely terminate script execution without a Python exception when the constraint is violated.

method_breakdown.summary: Renders a success banner with the arithmetic expression and result, plus a collapsible expander with structured computation metadata.
parameters: symbol (str): Unicode symbol corresponding to the operation ('+', '-', '×', '÷')
method_breakdown.significance: Closes the user feedback loop by presenting the result clearly and providing a drill-down detail view for transparency and debugging.

files.summary: Declares the sole runtime dependency: Streamlit version 1.40.0 or higher.
files.description: This file pins the minimum acceptable version of Streamlit required to run the application. It ensures reproducible installation across environments using 'pip install -r requirements.txt'. No transitive dependency pinning is performed; pip resolves sub-dependencies automatically.

files.summary: Setup and run instructions for the Streamlit Calculator App.
files.description: The README provides the minimum information a developer needs to get the application running locally: how to create and activate a Python virtual environment, how to install dependencies, and how to launch the Streamlit development server.

package_summary.structure: Flat single-package application (no sub-modules or packages)
